title: Human Urine on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30 minutes to several hours plus fan-dry; batting involvement = pro
stainChemistry: Human urine is urea + uric acid + urochrome (yellow pigment) + mild protein. Less concentrated than pet urine but the same odor-risk mechanism: bacteria metabolize urea into ammonia, raising pH and producing the characteristic smell. On upholstery, batting saturation is the invisible failure mode — bacteria trap in the cushion foam / batting and re-emit odor for weeks. Check the upholstery code (W / W-S / S / X) before any liquid touches the fabric.
whyThisWorks: Enzyme digests the urea and uric acid before bacteria can fully metabolize them. Vinegar rinse neutralizes residual ammonia. Baking soda dry dust + fan + time dry the batting enough to prevent ongoing bacterial activity. The limitation: batting soak = pro extraction needed (home chemistry can't reach the foam).
defaultAssumption: Fresh small spot on W / W-S upholstery = reasonable DIY. Larger spill or batting soak (accident on a couch cushion, mattress cover) = escalate early — home ceiling is low once padding is involved. Pet urine has its own card (pet-urine-upholstery) with different enzyme targeting.

spottingProtocol.technique: Read tag, extract as much as possible
spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on. Read upholstery code: W / W-S (water-based OK), S (solvent only — not for urine), X (vacuum only — call a pro). Press folded white towel hard on the spot to extract as much urine as possible BEFORE chemistry touches it.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold NSD via damp cloth. Press, lift, rotate. Never pour. Weighted dry towel 5 min on top to extract. 2–3 cycles.

spottingProtocol.technique: Damp cloth enzyme for protein + urea
spottingProtocol.equipment: Liquid Protein Spotter OR enzyme pretreat in warm water (30–38°C) on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen cloth with enzyme solution, press on spot, dwell 15–20 min. Digests urea + uric acid protein before bacteria can metabolize.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Equal parts white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar rinse via damp cloth. Neutralizes residual ammonia from bacterial breakdown. Blot dry.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Heavy dry towel + weight, baking soda
spottingProtocol.instruction: Weighted dry towel 30 min to pull moisture up from batting. Then dust baking soda over the area. Leave overnight. Vacuum next day.
Fan the area. Smell-check next day. If odor returns or a ring wicks back, batting is contaminated — call a pro.

steps: 1. Gloves. Code-tag check. S / X = pro. Blot extract hard.
2. Cold NSD damp cloth + weighted extract.
5. Weighted dry towel 30 min + baking soda dry dust overnight.
6. Fan-dry + smell-check next day. Batting odor = pro.
warnings: Never pour liquid on upholstery — batting saturation
Never use hot water — sets protein
Never ammonia-based cleaners — pets / kids smell them as urine

homeSolutions.instruction: Human urine on upholstery has a low pro-escalation threshold. If the spill soaked through (pooled more than a second), covers more than a dinner plate, or it's on a mattress or cushion you can't fully extract, call a pro. Home surface treatment can't reach batting, and that's where the smell persists.
Gloves on. Find the upholstery care code — usually under a cushion. W / W-S: proceed. S-only: solvent lane (not for urine) — call a pro. X: vacuum only — definitely a pro.
Press a thick stack of white paper towels or folded cloths on the spot. Lift, dry section, press. Get as much urine OUT as possible before any chemistry.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Cleaner on a Damp Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz) in WARM water per the label — or use a human-urine-specific cleaner if you have one. Dampen a cloth, press on the spot 15–20 minutes. Don't let it dry during dwell.
Mix equal parts white vinegar + cool water. Dab on a damp cloth. Press on the spot 5 minutes. Neutralizes residual ammonia — the step that most home guides skip.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel + Baking Soda Overnight
homeSolutions.instruction: Press a thick DRY towel with a heavy book on the spot for 30 minutes. Then dust baking soda generously over the area. Leave overnight with a fan pointed at it. Vacuum off next morning. Baking soda + airflow + time = dry batting = no returning smell.

homeSolutions.agent: Smell-Check Next Day, Escalate If Odor Returns
homeSolutions.instruction: Sniff the area next morning in daylight. Any lingering smell = batting is contaminated = pro call. Tell them: "Human urine on [W / W-S / code unknown] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated with enzyme + vinegar + baking soda + fan-dry. [Odor persists / cleared]."

steps: 1. Honest scope: batting soak / large area / S-X code = call a pro.
4. Enzyme pretreat on damp cloth 15–20 min.
6. Weighted dry towel + baking soda overnight.
7. Smell-check next day. Odor return = pro.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if upholstery code is S or X, if batting soak suspected, if large area, or if odor returns after a full home cycle.
warnings: NEVER pour — batting saturation is irreversible at home
Batting involvement = pro-only from that point

neverDo: Never pool liquid on upholstery — batting ruin
Never hot water — cooks protein + sets
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S upholstery: tolerates enzyme + vinegar + baking soda

materialWarnings: Pro-lane threshold is LOW on urine-upholstery
Batting contamination is invisible + permanent at home
Never ammonia cleaners — smells like urine

escalation.when: S / X code upholstery, wool, large spill, batting soak, or odor returns after a full home cycle.
escalation.whatToTell: "Human urine on [W / W-S / code unknown] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated with enzyme + vinegar + baking soda + fan-dry. [Odor persists / cleared / batting suspect]."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with hot-water extraction

contextQuestions: How deep did it soak — surface, through the fabric, into cushion?
How long ago — fresh, overnight, older?
Is this human or pet urine? (use pet-urine-upholstery for pet)
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Human urine is usually less concentrated than pet urine but the batting-contamination failure mode is the same. Low pro-escalation threshold. Ammonia-cleaner-avoidance still applies (even without pets).
